questions.question_text: Is our reported readmission rate of 13.3% accurate?
questions.answer_summary: If readmission rates are understated by even 2%, we risk CMS penalties exceeding $500K annually and board-level compliance exposure.

questions.question_text: Why are there near-duplicate encounter records in our data?
questions.answer_summary: Duplicate encounters inflate throughput metrics and distort per-encounter cost calculations, potentially misallocating $2M+ in department budgets.

questions.question_text: Which departments have the most unreliable data?
questions.answer_summary: Targeting remediation to the 2-3 worst departments yields 80% of data quality improvement at 20% of the cost of a system-wide overhaul.

questions.question_text: What should we fix first to improve our data reliability score?
questions.answer_summary: Addressing duplicate encounters alone would improve the reliability score by 15-20 points, making board-reported metrics defensible within one reporting cycle.

questions.question_text: Can we defend these numbers if the board challenges our charge capture totals?
questions.answer_summary: Current charge capture discrepancies of 5%+ mean we cannot certify reported revenue to the board without qualification, creating audit committee risk.
